message:
🐛 Unset CLAUDECODE env var in startup scripts (#1068)

When the console is started from within a Claude Code session, the
CLAUDECODE environment variable is inherited by the Go backend. This
causes AI Missions to fail with "Claude Code cannot be launched inside
another Claude Code session". Unsetting it in startup scripts allows
the backend to spawn claude-code for diagnose/repair missions.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -20,6 +20,7 @@ echo -e "${GREEN}=== KubeStellar Console - Demo Mode ===${NC}"
 echo ""
 
 # Environment
+unset CLAUDECODE  # Allow AI Missions to spawn claude-code even when started from a Claude Code session
 export DEV_MODE=true
 export SKIP_ONBOARDING=true
 export FRONTEND_URL=http://localhost:5174

@@ -144,6 +144,7 @@ if [ -z "$JWT_SECRET" ]; then
 fi
 
 # Environment
+unset CLAUDECODE  # Allow AI Missions to spawn claude-code even when started from a Claude Code session
 export SKIP_ONBOARDING=true
 if [ "$USE_DEV_SERVER" = true ]; then
     export DEV_MODE=true
